title: P1 fix: manifest examples must compose with invoke and run through the dispatcher
action: P1 fix (review-phase-1.md, corroborated by architecture + api-contract reviewers): rewrite every registry entry's examples[] as a full dispatcher-form command consistent with its own invoke string (e.g. cells.show: invoke='bee cells show', example='bee cells show --id demo-1 --json' - no duplicated action word). Update test_bee_cli.mjs's runExample to execute each example through bee.mjs itself (strip the leading 'bee' token, spawn bee.mjs with the rest) as the primary proof, and additionally derive legacy-helper-form args (strip 'bee <group> ' from the example, matching the entry's own group derived from its name) to keep the existing legacy-helper parity check.

truths: Every registry entry's example, when its invoke prefix is prepended/considered, is a directly runnable bee.mjs command with no duplicated tokens
Every example is executed through bee.mjs itself and asserted to succeed, not only through the legacy helper

artifacts.substantive: examples rewritten in dispatcher-form, no duplicated action tokens
runExample executes via bee.mjs, not only the legacy helper

key_links: runExample's dispatcher execution path is exercised for every registry entry
prohibitions: No change to invoke strings or to any command's actual parameter schema

trace.outcome: Rewrote all 21 registry examples in dispatcher-form (no duplicated action tokens); runExample now executes them through bee.mjs directly
